meaning: adj.鍚戜笢鍖楃殑,鏉ヨ嚜涓滃寳鐨刟dv.鍦ㄤ笢鍖楀湴
examples: Years of heavy spring and early summer rains, mild winters, and frequent Atlantic storms, or to periods of droughts, light northeasterly winds, and summer heat waves.
The next bit of the path led in a northeasterly ascent to the last part of Temple Valley.
The cloud had drifted in a northeasterly direction and occupied much of the airspace over the Gulf of Alaska.
When strong northeasterly winds picked up, the oil was dispersed beyond any hope of containment and wreaked havoc on the pristine ecosystem.
